title: The messed up licensing of Evangelion
display_title: The messed up licensing of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: The messed up licensing of Evangelion             Quote Post by Seen » Wed Oct 26, 2011 11:37 pm Might as well make a thread for the hell that is Eva licensing in general. Who holds the rights to it? Will it ever be released ever again? Is anyone even TRYING to get the rights to EOE? Will we ever get a redub?

[]:             Quote Post by SHINOBI-03 » Thu Oct 27, 2011 3:14 am I asked about the chance of a re-dub.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 27, 2011 8:28 am As far as North America is concerned: Sentai Filmworks (Formerly ADV Films) currently has the rights to Neon Genesis Evangelion TV and 21'-24'. Funimation currently has the rights to 1.01, 1.11, and 2.22. Viz Media currently has the rights to the Neon Genesis Evangelion manga by Yoshiyuki Sadamoto. Dark Horse Manga currently has the rights to the Ikari Shinji Raising Project manga and "Campus Apocalypse" manga. Lost in the fray: Manga Entertainment had the rights to Death & Rebirth and The End of Evangelion, but it has been confirmed that the licensed has expired. ADV Manga had the rights to the "Angelic Days" manga, however it is unknown what happened to those rights after ADV's restructuring/rebranding. ADV Films (which apparently still exists) had the rights to produce a Live Action Evangelion Movie, and is currently suing Gainax over breaching their legal agreement.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Thu Oct 27, 2011 11:04 am Some other companies to add to the mix: Chuang Yi based out of Singapore translates and distributes Sadamoto's manga, seemingly competing with Viz in Australia at least. The difference between the two being that Chuang Yi includes the dust jackets and full colour art pages at the front of every issue, where as Viz does not. Madman has re-distributed nearly every version of Evangelion in Australia including VHS, original DVDs, Platinum DVDs, Death and Rebirth, EoE, and Rebuild 1.01, 1.11 and 2.22. RedAnt in Australia released an early sub only version of 1.01 (mislabelled as 1.0) complete with Japanese rainbow credits. The rights for Angelic Days are probably still owned by some subsidiary of ADV, or are on the market at the moment considering how the books are now out of print. I've heard some talk around comic book shops when I was looking for copies of Angelic Days of hopes that Viz or Dark Horse will pick up the rights and start printing them again, but I'm not sure of their sources.
